findings.message: postinstall lifecycle hook auto-executes node scripts/postinstall.js on every npm install; script performs network download of a native binary and rewrites global bin symlinks/shims
symlinkSync replaces the agent-browser symlink in the npm global bin directory (system PATH location) at install time via fixUnixSymlink()
writeFileSync overwrites .cmd and .ps1 shims in the Windows global npm prefix dir outside the package directory via fixWindowsShims()
Downloads platform-specific native binary from GitHub Releases over HTTPS; URL constructed from package.json version field; no SHA256 checksum verification before chmodSync makes file executable
execSync invoked with shell command string 'ldd --version 2>&1 || true' for musl libc detection inside isMusl()
COMMAND=$* captures all user-supplied CLI args; value is passed via Python json.dump to AWS SSM and executed as PowerShell on a remote EC2 instance; intentional developer debug tool
allowed-tools declares Bash(npx agent-browser:*) but line 25 of the body explicitly prohibits npx: 'Always use agent-browser directly -- never npx agent-browser'

No allowed-tools field in frontmatter; all five other SKILL.md files declare Bash(agent-browser:*) and Bash(npx agent-browser:*); omission may be intentional since skill teaches TypeScript SDK patterns rather than direct CLI invocations
